schedules.title: Schedule 9 – Delivery performance (milestones)
items: Site establishment, safety and access preparation
Removal of redundant equipment (fans, exchangers etc.)
Pouring and curing of concrete plinths for chillers
Delivery and installation of new chillers
AHU 1 & 3 internal plenum modification
Installation of coils, drift eliminators, and sumps
Supply & install EC fans and new access panels
Pipework and ductwork modifications
Electrical upgrades and cabling works
BMS integration and BACnet configuration
Testing and commissioning
Handover, documentation, and practical completion
12-month defect liability and maintenance period begins

schedules.title: Schedule 10 – Fee Structure (pricing items)
items: 1.0. Removal and disposal of redundant services including fans and exchangers
2.0. Supply, deliver and install (2) water-cooled chillers with inbuilt pumps and BACnet HLI card
3.0. Supply, deliver and install (8) EC fans, doors, panels, louvres
4.0. Supply and install coils: evaporator (4), condenser (2), run-around (4), and pumps (2)
5.0. Supply and install insulated copper pipes & fittings with metal cladding and all associated valves
6.0. Modify, supply and install exhaust ductwork
7.0. Modify and make good AHUs’ internal plenum, rust treatment, patching
8.0. All electrical works in Plantroom 1 for AHU 1 & 3 upgrades
9.0. Project preliminaries and documentation
10.0. Maintenance for twelve months

sections.title: Overview
items: Includes new/replacement heat pump chillers and AHU refurbishments

sections.title: Technical Scope Summary
items: Supply & install x2 Water-cooled heat pump chiller
4 new evaporator coils
2 condenser coils
2 sets of run-around heat recovery coils with pumps/VSDs
8 EC plug exhaust fans (≈4 per AHU)
Pump duty flow: 7.9 L/s
Pump duty head: 10.0 m

Provide product data/catalogues & technical sheets for all proposed equipment
Provide draft layout and connection diagrams
Provide methodology/program and risk controls
Provide warranty terms and service schedules

testing_commissioning:
All wiring penetrating a fire wall shall be installed using a special fire rated intumescent fitting. 
10. TESTING & COMMISSIONING 
10.1 General 
Provide all equipment, labour, materials, power, consumables, etc. to conduct testing and 
commissioning. The Contractor shall carry out his own tests to verify operation and performance 
before requesting Council to attend inspections. Submit fully detailed Test Log sheets for inspection 
prior to any testing taking place. The Test Log sheets shall be A4 in size and present a clear and 
precise record of all tests to be carried out. 
10.2 Testing 
The following tests and checks shall be carried out by the Contractor: 
1. Visual inspection of pipework and joints for leaks. 
2. Water-test drains and overflow piping for leaks and for unobstructed drainage. 
3. Check all power cabling terminations and megger test each circuit. 
4. Verify integrity of all controls cabling to ensure electrical continuity and freedom from shorts and 
grounds. 
5. Sequence checks of all specified control functions and cycles, such as Start, Stop, Chiller Staging, 
Cooling and Heating Cycles, etc.

of a type test certificate from a qualified testing laboratory. 
 
The section for switching of sub-system, circuitry shall be labelled 'Authorised Personnel Only", this 
section shall not allow any person operating switches and circuit breakers to come into contact with 
live parts. 
 
All other sections of the SCA shall be labelled as indicated on switchboard drawings. 
LED pilot light "Run" and "Fault" indication for all main components will be provided including 
supply and exhaust fans. Lamp test button is also required. 
 
Individual pilot light 'fault' indication for all safety controls will be provided. 
9.3 Connections 
All cables unless enclosed shall be neatly arranged and loosely tied and supported neatly, where 
necessary. 
All control cables to and from the switchboards shall terminate at an approved terminal block. No more 
than two cables shall terminate at any one terminal point. Power cables for Compressor and 
Condenser motors may be directly terminated on respective Contactors/over-loads.

on a plinth with SS Tray under the feet and drained to waste will be provided for each closed system. 
A set of corrosion coupons are to be provided for each closed system and tested each three months. 
9. ELECTRICAL 
9.1 Requirements 
All equipment supplied and all work done shall be in accordance with this specification and the S.A.A. 
Wiring Rules and any other S.A.A. publications covering design, manufacture or installation of 
electrical equipment and to the requirements of the local Electricity Supply Authority. The latest issue 
or amendment of the relevant S.A.A. publication shall be used. 
The switchboard manufacturer shall have a type test certificate in addition to Part Type Test Assembly 
certificate from switchgear supplier to which the design, manufacture and performance of the 
switchboard shall conform. The installation Contractor shall have an Electrical Contractors license and 
employ only licensed electricians on Site. 
9.2 Switchboards 
The S.C.A. shall comply in all respects with AS 61439. 
Proof of performance of the board shall be substantiated by switchboard manufacturers’ submission 
of a type test certificate from a qualified testing laboratory.
warranty:
BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments                                                                     Page 19 of 20 
 
11. WARRANTY  
Warranties for installation and equipment shall be for a period of 12 months minimum. 
12. MAINTENANCE & SERVICE 
The Installation Contractor is responsible for carrying out all preventative maintenance and servicing 
for the duration of the Defects Liability Period.  
The mechanical contractor shall allow in the tender for maintenance and servicing from date of 
practical completion to the end of the maintenance and defects liability period, to comply with 
requirements of AS 3666, Local Authority and any other statutory body having jurisdiction. 
Maintenance, in addition to rectification of faults, emergency service and carrying out of capacity 
tests as required, shall also include a minimum of 12 regular monthly visits for routine maintenance.

bmcs:
sheath at low level pipework (under 1.8m).  
2.2.3 Upgrade of Building Management Control System & Associated Works (by 
Others) 
Note: HVAC and BMCS contractors shall be responsible for coordinating inter-related works and no 
waver of responsibility is allowed. Advise the project officer any conflict between the scope and 
responsibility. 
a. Extend the existing BMCS to control the new works, logic shall be identical to the newly 
refurbished AHU 2 control 
b. Supply and install necessary and additional points to control and monitor the new equipment  
c. Re-engineer and reprogram BMCS and other necessary works to completely control the new 
ventilation and heating systems  
d. Integrate the new Chiller BACnet High Level Interface gateway with the BMS to operate as 
specified and provide the Device Instance and IP details and shall carry out all necessary 
programming to ensure the current Chiller Staging strategy is maintained. 
e. Test and commission the new installation. 
f. Allow progressive fine-tuning of control logic after Commissioning up to the end of the 
Defects Liability Period, without any additional cost to the Contract.

BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments                                                                     Page 12 of 20 
 
3.1.9 Controls 
The man-machine-interface shall be via a 180mm (nominal 7”) touch screen, finger or touch-pencil 
capable. The control system shall be similar to Carrier “Touch Pilot” with the “Energy Management 
Module”, or approved equivalent. 
The touch screen shall permit web-connectivity, BACnet IP connectivity, trending capability and 
downloadable documentation capability. 
BMS Interface shall be factory installed to provide a high-level interface (HLI - BACnet over IP 
Gateway) with the existing BMS. This shall enable the BMS system to interrogate the control panel, 
reset the chiller setpoints, and manage alarms. Allow for necessary field programming. 
Set the Device Instance and IP details and provide a mapping table for the HLI points. 
The following safeties shall be provided: 
1. Reverse rotation (phase reversal). 
2. Low Water Temperature. 
3. Low Water Flow Rate. 
4. Motor Thermal Overload. 
5. High Pressure Cut-out. 
6. Low Suction Pressure. 
7. Limit Start Timer. 
8. Electrical overload. 
9. Loss of Phase.
equipment:
tested, evacuated and fully factory charged with refrigerant. The chiller shall be factory tested at its 
full thermal load and each capacity step. 
The chiller shall consist of scroll type compressors with soft start capability, condenser, evaporator, 
microprocessor controls and user display. The chiller shall be complete with a factory installed main 
circuit breaker with electrical disconnect/isolation capability. 
The chiller shall incorporate in-built chilled and condenser water pumps sized to handle the chiller’s 
total nominated water flow at least 400kPA capacity driven by a VSD and controlled by the chiller’s 
internal controller. 
Service availability, technical support and spare parts shall be readily available in Australia. The 
manufacturer’s recommendations shall be followed in regards to handling, installation, commissioning 
and ongoing maintenance. The chiller manufacturer’s local distributor shall be engaged by the 
Contractor for maintenance during the Defects Liability Period. 
3.1.2 Product Data 
Provide the following information with the Tender: 
1. Manufacturer’s rated performance data for the Chiller offered.

Main Switch room into existing MSSB-1 located in Plantroom 1. 
d. Supply and install switchgears in MSSB-1 to accommodate new chillers and provide power 
to the new sub-board, including terminals and relays for BMS interfacing, ensuring correct

e. Supply and install new electrical sub-board, cables and circuit breakers for the auxiliary

f. Supply and install cable tray, cable supports, cabling, isolator and connection where required  
g. Supply and installation of cable support, cabling, isolator and connection to (8) Exhaust Fans 
in the AHUs and the new chillers.  
h. Supply and install 7.5kW ABB ACH580 VSD’s to respective circulating pumps for the heat 
recovery coils.

and suitable for the operating weight of the Chiller. Minimum vibration isolation efficiency shall be 
95%. 
3.1.5 Compressors 
Compressors shall be of the full-hermetic scroll type with integral oil level sight glass. Each 
compressor shall be driven by a 2-pole electric motor cooled by suction gas and protected by internal 
temperature sensors. Over temperature motor protection shall also be provided. 
Each compressor shall be fitted with “soft start” control complete with phase loss protection. Each 
compressor shall be fitted with an electric crankcase heater. 
Each compressor assembly shall be anti-vibration mounted to the chiller frame. 
Each refrigerant circuit shall be complete with the following: 
1. Suction and discharge line isolation valves. 
2. Replaceable core filter drier. 
3. Moisture indicating sight glass. 
4. Electronic expansion device. 
5. Liquid line service valves. 
3.1.6 Evaporator Heat Exchanger 
The evaporator shall be a plate heat exchanger type fabricated from Type 316L stainless steel, copper 
brazed. Evaporators shall be overall insulated with 19mm closed cell foam type insulation. Evaporator

 Programming of the Work 
Due to the nature of the work it is brought to the Contractors’ attention that programming of the work 
and subsequent site management of the work are considered essential to minimise disruption to the 
leisure centre’s activities. 
Immediately following award, the Contractor shall submit to Council a detailed schedule showing the 
proposed sequence of work and the durations for which the various areas will need to be available to 
the Contractor for the work to be completed. This shall be based on the schedule and time for 
completion submitted with the Tender. 
1.5 
Centre’s Opening Hours 
Leisure centre’s hours are 6:00am to 9:00pm daily. Any noisy works shall consider the neighbouring 
properties such as any noisy works shall be limited to typical working hours or agreed with the leisure 
centre’s management.
